title: A spring poem from bion by Eugene Field — Love Poetry Lines
content:
One asketh:
"Tell me, Myrson, tell me true:
What's the season pleaseth you?
Is it summer suits you best,
When from harvest toil we rest?
Is it autumn with its glory
Of all surfeited desires?
Is it winter, when with story
meaning: "A spring poem from bion" by Eugene Field is a love and deep and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 34 lines. The poet explores themes of love and deep and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "One asketh: "Tell me, Myrson, tell me true:...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Eugene Field's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
